title: Platform Architecture and Authentication Foundation
objective: Establish the baseline platform architecture, service boundaries, API contracts, and secure authentication for a multi-user options platform.
scope: Define monolith-to-microservices migration plan with clear service contracts.
Implement authentication and role model (admin, trader).
Set API gateway rules for internal and external traffic.
Define message-bus event schemas for analytics, recommendation, and execution flows.
deliverables: Architecture decision record (ADR) for service decomposition.
Auth service design and token policy (JWT/session, expiry, rotation).
Initial service template with health, logging, tracing hooks.
success_metrics: All downstream services consume standardized identity claims.
No unauthenticated access to admin-only operations.
Service interfaces agreed before implementation of W02-W08.
risks: Unclear role permissions for strategy marketplace actions.
open_questions: Should API gateway be implemented now or deferred until full microservice split?
Do users require broker-level RBAC or only platform-level RBAC?
Should refresh tokens be persistent (DB) or stateless?
